dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used dc_upper (Donchian upper) for 20-period highest high. Used vol_ratio_20 to represent volume expansion relative to 20-period average (scaled to 0-1, so 0.75 = 75% above average). Exit conditions capture price closing below entry on declining volume using hvol_20 as the volume baseline reference.

backtest_rule.reason: Used dc_upper (Donchian upper) for 20-period highest high. Used vol_ratio_20 to represent volume expansion relative to 20-period average (scaled to 0-1, so 0.75 = 75% above average). Exit conditions capture price closing below entry on declining volume using hvol_20 as the volume baseline reference.

source_rule_text: A trader should initiate long positions when price enters new highs accompanied by volume expansion of at least 50% above recent average daily volume, as this signals genuine demand and readiness to move higher.
